name: Transactional Outbox
def: Writing events to an outbox table in the same DB transaction as state, then relaying them reliably.
problem: Avoid the dual-write problem: updating a database and publishing a message atomically.
mechanism: The state change and an outbox row commit in one local transaction; a separate relay (poller or CDC) reads the outbox and publishes to the broker with at-least-once delivery.
benefits: No lost messages on crash between DB and broker
failures: Dual write without outbox losing events
myths: "Just publish after commit" — a crash in between loses the event; the outbox prevents that
